title: Express 5 Resource Design, Middleware Order, Async Errors, and Cancellation · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs express 5 resource design, middleware order, async errors, and cancellation from source and emitted revision, tenant, URL, validator, bytes, runtime validation, Promise settlement, transaction, lease, response, failure, handle cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for layered Express 5 feed-reader API after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Design sources, subscriptions, entries, timelines, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions and method semantics.
Order authority, request identity, limits, authentication, negotiation, validation, authorization, handlers, not-found, and error middleware explicitly.
Use Express 5 Promise rejection flow while preventing double next, double send, partial response, and lost unknown causes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Feed Ts Api Resource Decisions · changed case · Review Feed Ts Api…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 9379 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile resource decisions, middleware order, async errors, request abort, and response finality; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, entity, date, identity, or runtime-validation case and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Design sources, subscriptions, entries, timelines, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions and method semantics.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, cast one convenient XML object as a feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, source and emitted revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, entity and dangerous-key limits, unknown runtime validation, Fetch stream and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL client, Promise settlement, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as feed-service proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Feed Ts Response Finality, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
